data_origin_policy: Only values from documented project sources and previous extraction are included.

sources.title: Nutritional Guidelines for Feeding Pet Rabbits
sources.notes: Main source for rabbit_adult_maintenance numeric nutrient requirements. Values are for total diet at 12% moisture and assumed 10 MJ DE/kg when stated.

sources.title: Nutrition of Rabbits
sources.notes: Clinical synthesis. Useful for maintenance values, water intake, pellet amount, calcium and fibre practical values.

sources.title: L'alimentation chez le lapin
sources.notes: Francophone clinical source with concrete adult pellet thresholds, greens, water and treat quantities.

sources.title: Caring for Your Pet Rabbit
sources.notes: Useful for diet fraction, pellet quantity and greens quantity. Mostly owner-facing practical data.

sources.title: Alfalfa hay
sources.notes: Ingredient composition values only. Do not store as rabbit requirements.

animal_profiles.description: Profil initial pour lapin adulte non gestant, non lactant, sans pathologie connue.

ingredient_compositions.notes: Stored as lignin/ADL equivalent according to extraction source label.

do_not_integrate.reason: Aucune valeur adulte chiffrée exploitable trouvée dans les sources retenues.
Pas de besoin chiffré direct, sauf base FEDIAF à 12 % d'humidité et eau en ml/kg/jour.
Pas de besoin adulte chiffré direct.

normalization_notes.note: Always compare product values only when unit and basis are compatible. For example, % as_fed should not be compared blindly against % dry_matter or feed_12pct_moisture_total_diet.
For rabbit_adult_maintenance nutrient scoring, FEDIAF rows with priority=primary should be preferred over secondary clinical summaries when conflicts exist.
Feedipedia alfalfa values are ingredient composition records. They are not rabbit requirements.
review_status=extracted_from_source means extracted and structured from documented sources. It is not a veterinary sign-off.

comparison_logic: Filter nutrient_requirements by species_id and animal_profile_id.
Prefer priority=primary and requirement_type in nutrient_requirement, safe_upper_limit, water_requirement.
Do not use ingredient_compositions as requirements.
Check unit and basis before comparing values.
When only a target_value exists, treat as target, not min or max.
When max_value only exists, treat excess as a risk flag.
When min_value only exists, treat deficit as a risk flag.
When min_value and max_value both exist, treat values inside range as acceptable, below/above as warnings.
Use practical_ration rows for feeding guidance, not for chemical score unless product_type matches.
